quotes.text: On August 14, 2016, J. P. Morgan and Evercore sent a written instruction letter to each Interested Party, requiring a definitive written

On August 14, 2016, J. P. Morgan and Evercore sent a written instruction letter to each Interested Party, requiring a definitive written proposal by 12:00 p. m. Pacific Time on Friday, August 19, 2016. The instructions also requested that each Interested Party submit any comments on the draft of the merger agreement by 5:00 p. m. Pacific Time on Thursday, August 18, 2016.

On Friday, August 19, 2016, each of Pfizer, Company 1, Company 2, and Company 3 submitted a definitive proposal for an all-cash acquisition of Medivation. Company 4 declined to submit a definitive proposal and

In light of the foregoing, the Medivation board was of the view that it should seek to control any process to explore the value that might be obtained in a transaction with a third party. Accordingly, the Medivation board directed J. P. Morgan and Evercore to contact select industry participants, to determine respective levels of preliminary interest in a strategic process, with the objective of expeditiously entering into confidentiality

On June 27, 2016, Medivation received a private letter with an increased proposal from Sanofi of $58.00 per share in cash plus a contingent value right ( CVR) of up to $3.00 per share, conditioned on certain revenue metrics.
Medivation entered into confidentiality agreements with Pfizer and one additional party initially contacted by Medivations financial advisors.

On July 5, 2016, Medivation entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sanofi, pursuant to which Sanofi agreed to terminate its Consent Solicitation and be bound by a six-month standstill, subject to limited termination events, in order to facilitate friendly confidential discussions and a due diligence investigation.

On July 9, 2016, Medivation entered into a confidentiality agreement with a fourth industry participant initially contacted by Medivation's financial advisors.

Company 4 submitted a preliminary proposal to acquire Medivation for $63.00 per Share in cash, plus CVRs of up to $5.00 per Share conditioned on certain regulatory milestones for pipeline assets.

On August 12, 2016, Company 2 and Company 3 submitted written revised proposals for an all-cash acquisition of Medivation, at $70.00 per Share and $70.50 per Share, respectively. Later that day, Medivations financial advisors provided Company 2 and Company 3 with the draft merger agreement and access to the second stage of confirmatory diligence.

The definitive proposals, in order of increasing valuation, were from Company 3 for $72.50 per Share in cash, Company 1 for $73.00 per Share in cash, Company 2 for $75.50 per Share in cash, and Pfizer for $77.00 per Share in cash. Together with their definitive proposals, each of Company 1 and Company 3 also submitted a revised version of the merger agreement.

Also on August 20, 2016, following approval of the Medivation board of directors, Pfizer and Medivation executed the merger agreement and Medivation executed the Rights Amendment. On August 22, 2016, the parties issued a joint press release announcing the transaction.

observations.summary: J. P. Morgan and Evercore sent a written instruction letter requiring definitive written proposals from the Interested Parties by August 19, 2016.
J. P. Morgan and Evercore sent a written instruction letter requiring definitive written proposals from the Interested Parties by August 19, 2016.
Medivation's financial advisors told the remaining Interested Parties that best and final proposals were due by noon on August 20, 2016.
Medivation's financial advisors told the remaining Interested Parties that best and final proposals were due by noon on August 20, 2016.
Sanofi made an unsolicited approach and requested a telephone discussion with Medivation.
Medivation identified J. P. Morgan as retained as its independent financial advisor at the March 24 special meeting.
Sanofi submitted a non-binding proposal to acquire Medivation for $52.50 per share in cash.
Pfizer indicated interest in participating in any strategic discussions Medivation might initiate.
Medivation engaged Evercore as an additional independent financial advisor.
Sanofi publicly announced its proposal in a press release.
Medivation had retained Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz as an additional legal advisor by the April 28 special meeting.
Pfizer reiterated its interest in participating in any strategic discussions Medivation might initiate.
Sanofi restated its proposal in a press release and threatened a consent solicitation.
Sanofi sought a confidentiality agreement and due diligence access for a potential strategic transaction.
Pfizer reiterated interest in a negotiated transaction process with Medivation.
Medivation's board launched a controlled strategic process and directed J. P. Morgan and Evercore to contact select industry participants.
Sanofi submitted an increased proposal of $58.00 per share in cash plus a CVR of up to $3.00 per share.
Pfizer entered into a confidentiality agreement with Medivation.
Sanofi entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Medivation.
Pfizer's management team expressed continued interest in exploring a negotiated strategic transaction with Medivation.
Medivation entered into a confidentiality agreement with a fourth industry participant initially contacted by its financial advisors.

date.resolution_note: Year inferred from the surrounding July 2016 chronology.

observations.summary: Medivation entered into a confidentiality agreement with a fifth industry participant initially contacted by its financial advisors.
Pfizer submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal for an all-cash acquisition at $65.00 per share.
Company 1 submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal for an all-cash acquisition at $71.00 per share.
Company 2 submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal for an all-cash acquisition at $62.00 to $64.00 per share.
Company 3 submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal of $60.00 per share in cash plus CVRs of up to $10.00 per share.
Company 4 submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal of $63.00 per share in cash plus CVRs of up to $5.00 per share.
Company 2 communicated by telephone that it would submit a revised all-cash proposal at $70.00 per share.
Company 2 submitted a written revised all-cash proposal at $70.00 per share.
Company 3 submitted a written revised all-cash proposal at $70.50 per share.
Company 3 submitted a definitive all-cash proposal at $72.50 per share.
Company 1 submitted a definitive all-cash proposal at $73.00 per share.
Company 2 submitted a definitive all-cash proposal at $75.50 per share.
Pfizer submitted a definitive all-cash proposal at $77.00 per share.
Pfizer submitted a revised final proposal at $81.50 per share.
Company 1 submitted a revised final proposal at $80.25 per share.
Company 2 submitted a revised final proposal at $80.00 per share.
Medivation executed the merger agreement with Pfizer.
Medivation executed the merger agreement with Pfizer.
The parties issued a joint press release announcing the transaction.

exclusions.explanation: The filing references additional unnamed industry participants and a twelfth contacted party that cannot be linked confidently to specific actor_ids in the locked roster.
The filing later relabels the four non-Pfizer NDA counterparties as Company 1-4 but does not identify which specific Company 1-4 actor corresponds to the June 29, July 9, or July 13 execution dates.
